history.qwen_observation: The build failed on JDK 8 because maven-compiler-plugin passed the --release flag, which is unsupported by JDK 8's javac.
history.qwen_rationale: Switching the first step to JDK 11 avoids the JDK 8 --release incompatibility, and upgrading the compiler plugin to 3.13.0 ensures modern flag handling. Explicitly setting java.version and maven.compiler.release to 8 stabilizes the initial compile before the Java 11 migration step takes over.

history.qwen_rationale: Adding explicit JAXB dependencies (jaxb-api and jaxb-runtime) to the project classpath will resolve the missing class error, allowing the project to compile successfully on JDK 11 before proceeding with the Java version upgrades.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed at step 1 because the inherited maven-compiler-plugin:3.8.0 lacks JAXB on JDK 11, and the UpgradePluginVersion recipe failed to override the parent-managed version.
history.qwen_rationale: Running the initial plugin upgrade under JDK 8 avoids the immediate JAXB crash. Explicitly setting the maven-compiler-plugin.version property alongside the plugin version upgrade ensures the effective POM uses a JDK 11-compatible compiler (3.11.0) before any JDK 11+ compilation steps execute, bypassing the inheritance override issue.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed on JDK 11 because maven-compiler-plugin:3.8.0 (or its transitive dependency javassist:3.20.0-GA) crashed with NoClassDefFoundError for javax.xml.bind.JAXBException, which was removed from the JDK in version 11.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding explicit javax.xml.bind:jaxb-api and org.glassfish.jaxb:jaxb-runtime dependencies to the classpath will satisfy javassist's runtime requirements on JDK 11, allowing the initial compilation to succeed. Upgrading javassist directly provides an additional safety net. Once the project compiles on JDK 11, the subsequent steps can proceed to migrate to Java 17 and 21 without hitting the same classpath blocker.
